title: iam-jit users config (file mode)
description: Format for the users.yaml file when UserConfigSource=file. The same shape is produced by `iam-jit users export` and consumed by `iam-jit users import`.

schema_version.description: Schema version. Bumped on breaking changes; readers reject unknown versions.

auth_mode.description: Optional. If present, must match the deployment's AuthMode (server rejects on mismatch). Helps catch the case where a file built for one mode is uploaded to a deployment in the other mode.

id.description: User identifier. Local mode: 'email:<email>'. aws_iam mode: 'iam:<full-arn>'.

enabled.description: Set to false to disable a user without removing them. Disabled users get 401.

display_name.description: Human-readable name shown in the UI and audit log.

notes.description: Free-form notes for admin reference (e.g., 'left team 2026-08'). Not surfaced to non-admins.

outstanding_request_cap.description: #613 — per-user override of the outstanding-request cap. When omitted, deployment falls back to IAM_JIT_MAX_OUTSTANDING_PER_USER env or the built-in default (20). Pending+provisioning are counted; terminal states are not. Set to 0 to refuse all submissions from this user.
